title: Skip rhel-release branch unnecessary CI tasks
body:
RHEL release-branches potentially need to be maintained for a very long time.  Improve reliability and CI-speed by skipping tasks which are not needed for RHEL.  For example, there will (likely) never be RHEL releases of the MacOS or Windows versions of podman-remote.  Also, relocate the `win_installer_task` in the config. to better reflect it's sequence among CI tasks.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
***Review notes:***
* This is in response to the mass windows-task failures the other week on `v4.4.2-rhel` at a time when Jason was not available.  [Example](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6277362476646400)
* These changes should not have any impact on PRs to `main` or non-rhel release branches.
* I pushed these changes (plus a necessary test-commit) to [a dummy rhel-release branch](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/v99.99.99-rhel_ci_test_do_not_merge).  [You can see the CI run in cirrus](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6000046538227712).
* I attempted to add `only_ifs` to the debian and rawhide tasks, however this (somehow) greatly confused Cirrus-CI dependency-chasing logic, [causing many failures](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/4544162985410560).

`.cirrus.yml` env. vars quick reference:

* `$CIRRUS_BRANCH` is set to either `pull/<#>` (for PRs) or the name of the branch a job is running against (i.e. `vX.Y.Z-rhel`)
* `$CIRRUS_BASE_BRANCH` is only non-empty for PRs.  It's set to the base-branch name - for example, this could be a back-port PR fixing a CVE on a RHEL release branch.

Ahh darn, [the release-test task](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6382082302148608) is being triggered because the word "release" is in the title :disappointed: I can fix it if I need to re-push any changes to the PR.
